column1: # dot-secrets manifest — the MAP of vault items to local destinations.
# This file is tracked & stowed (it holds no secrets, only the wiring).
# Consumed by ~/bin/dot-secrets. Stows to ~/.config/dot-secrets/manifest.tsv.
# Columns (whitespace-separated; '#' and blank lines ignored):
#   kind   bw-item   source   dest   mode
#   kind   = field | note | attach
#     field  <name>      -> login password if name is "password", else custom field <name>
#     note   -           -> the item's Secure Note body (.notes); use '-' for source
#     attach <filename>  -> the item's attachment named <filename>, written to dest
#   bw-item = Bitwarden/Vaultwarden item name (one item per secret)
#   source  = field name / attachment filename / '-' for note
#   dest    = absolute path; a leading ~/ expands to $HOME
#   mode    = chmod applied after writing (always restrictive)
# Expected Vaultwarden items (populate these, then `dot-secrets sync`):
#   fastmail-dotfiles  login : password = Fastmail app password
#                      field : jmap-token = Fastmail JMAP API token
#                      (named -dotfiles to avoid clashing with the web-login item)
#   gh-hosts       attach : hosts.yml  = GitHub CLI ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml
#   git-local      note   : body = the per-host git signing overlay
# Rows whose item is absent from the vault are skipped (a host without mutt
# access just skips the fastmail rows). Re-running is idempotent.
# kind     bw-item        source      dest                               mode
field      fastmail-dotfiles  password    ~/.config/fastmail/imap-password   0600
field      fastmail-dotfiles  jmap-token  ~/.config/fastmail/tokens.txt      0600
attach     gh-hosts       hosts.yml   ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml             0600
note       git-local      -           ~/.config/git/local                0600
